error.signature: Bcrypt accepts password but silently ignores characters beyond 72 bytes

verdict.summary: Bcrypt has a hard 72-byte input limit (not characters - bytes). UTF-8 passwords with multibyte characters hit this limit sooner. Two passwords that share the first 72 bytes but differ after are treated as identical.

dead_ends.action: Use bcrypt for passwords of any length without pre-processing
dead_ends.why_fails: Passwords longer than 72 bytes are silently truncated. 'MySecurePassword123!+extra100characters' and 'MySecurePassword123!+extra100different' hash identically.

dead_ends.action: Set a maximum password length of 72 characters
dead_ends.why_fails: 72 bytes != 72 characters for UTF-8. A password with CJK characters or emojis uses 3-4 bytes per character, hitting the 72-byte limit at 18-24 characters.

workarounds.action: Pre-hash password with SHA-256 before bcrypt to handle arbitrary lengths
Use SHA-256 pre-hash and encode as base64 to stay within 72 bytes
Consider Argon2id which has no practical input length limit
workarounds.how: from argon2 import PasswordHasher; ph = PasswordHasher(); hash = ph.hash(password)  # no truncation
